name: Tres Picos – circular by the viewpoints of Ojén
name_original: Tres Picos – circular por los miradores de Ojén
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Sierra Blanca are found in a karstic mountain range in Andalusia, Spain. This region is characterized by pale grey limestone rock formations, extensive river valleys, and offers views of the Mediterranean Sea. The highest point reaches 4,183 feet (1,270 m). The area provides a network of trails suitable for various fitness levels. Best easy hiking trails in Sierra Blanca The most popular easy hiking route is Río Verde Ford – Charco del Canalón Waterfall loop from Majal de la Martina, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 17 minutes to complete. This route leads through natural wonders, including a waterfall and tranquil river sections. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ojén Caves – Ojén loop from Ojén, an easy 2.6 miles (4.1 km) path. This trail leads through varied terrain near the charming town of Ojén. Local hikers also love the Corzo Viewpoint – Macho Montes Viewpoint loop from Refugio de Juanar, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through scenic viewpoints and gentle ascents, often completed in about 1 hour 36 minutes. Hiking in Sierra Blanca is defined by karstic limestone formations, river valleys, and Mediterranean views. The network offers options for different ability levels, with many easy hiking trails available. The routes in Sierra Blanca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 2,400 reviews. More than 18,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierra Blanca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Sierra Blanca?
A: Sierra Blanca offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 160 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, there are more than 340 hiking routes across the region, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly easy hiking trails in Sierra Blanca?
A: Yes, many easy trails in Sierra Blanca are suitable for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14573961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojén Caves – Ojén loop from Ojén&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.6 miles (4.1 km) long, offer varied terrain near the charming town of Ojén and are often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes, making them great for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on easy hikes in Sierra Blanca?
A: While many trails in Sierra Blanca are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to check specific route regulations, especially within natural parks or protected areas. Generally, dogs are welcome on leashes on most public trails. Ensure you carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Are there easy circular hiking routes in Sierra Blanca?
A: Yes, Sierra Blanca features several easy circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33846196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corzo Viewpoint – Macho Montes Viewpoint loop from Ojén&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.0 miles (6.4 km) and offers scenic viewpoints and gentle ascents. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1065964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corzo Viewpoint – Macho Montes Viewpoint loop from Refugio de Juanar&lt;/a&gt;, covering 5.75 km.

Q: Are there easy trails that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31494509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Verde Ford – Charco del Canalón Waterfall loop from Majal de la Martina&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path that leads through natural wonders, including a waterfall and tranquil river sections. The Sierra Blanca in Andalusia is known for picturesque waterfalls and serene Pou Clar natural pools.

Q: What kind of viewpoints or attractions can I find on easy hikes in Sierra Blanca?
A: Easy hikes in Sierra Blanca often lead to stunning viewpoints and interesting attractions. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/892243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corzo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3162044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Gitanos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/411264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Monks' Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; also provide an excellent hiking destination with historical interest.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy hiking trails in Sierra Blanca?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting near towns like Ojén or Marbella's hinterland, designated parking areas are often available. For trails deeper in the wilderness, roadside parking might be an option, but it's always best to check specific route details or local signage before you go.

Q: Is public transport available to access easy hiking trails in Sierra Blanca?
A: Access to trailheads via public transport can be limited, especially for more remote routes. However, towns like Ojén, which serve as popular starting points for many trails, are generally accessible by local bus services. For trails in New Mexico, public transport options are less common, and a car is usually recommended.

Q: What is the best season for easy hiking in Sierra Blanca?
A: The best season for easy hiking depends on which Sierra Blanca region you plan to visit. In Andalusia, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. In New Mexico, late spring through early autumn is ideal, as higher elevations can be snow-capped well into summer. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What should I wear for easy hikes in Sierra Blanca?
A: For easy hikes in Sierra Blanca, comfortable, layered clothing is recommended to adapt to changing temperatures. Sturdy walking shoes or hiking boots are essential, even for shorter trails, due to varied terrain. Don't forget sun protection (hat, sunscreen), water, and a light backpack for essentials.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the easy hiking trails?
A: In the Sierra Blanca of Andalusia, charming towns like Ojén and Marbella's hinterland offer cafes, restaurants, and local shops where you can find refreshments and meals before or after your hike. For the Sierra Blanca in New Mexico, amenities are more spread out, so it's advisable to pack snacks and drinks, especially for trails further from Ski Apache or Ruidoso.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Sierra Blanca?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the karstic limestone formations in Spain to the alpine forests in New Mexico, as well as the breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea or the Tularosa Basin.

Q: Are there any easy trails that cross streams or offer unique natural features?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21816306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stream Crossing on the Trail loop from Altos de Xarblanca&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.1 km route that features a stream crossing, adding a unique element to your hike. The region's varied terrain often includes tranquil river sections and exposed pale grey limestone rock formations, particularly in the Spanish Sierra Blanca.
tips:
Great view from up there.
Were there in the summer. Set off extra early and packed plenty of water. Otherwise it would hardly have been possible.
Viewpoint of the Iberian Ibex. A sculpture of the ibex is enthroned above the vantage point - at first you wince, but quickly realize that the "animal" is not moving. :-D

The top of the Tajo Negro at 1085 masl has one of the best views towards the coast of Marbella and the interior of the Sierra de las Nieves Park. It is very close to other viewpoints that you can combine on your tour.
